reasoning_model_1: The model failed to execute the command to read the specific target sentence 'Today's class was truly…so…boring.'. Instead, it read the user's instruction prompt itself ('Please read this sentence with a dragon rhythm'). Due to this severe hallucination/instruction-following failure, the content accuracy is completely incorrect (Score 0). Consequently, the target paralinguistic feature (dragging rhythm) cannot be evaluated on the intended text.
reasoning_model_2: The model attempted to generate speech starting with 'Today', but immediately devolved into unintelligible gibberish and hallucinations ('Today wise class wealthy, su lee a su woo...'). The content is completely inconsistent with the script 'Today's class was truly…so…boring.'. The audio quality represents a severe model failure with broken prosody and wrong words. Therefore, it receives the lowest score based on the content accuracy criterion.
model_comparison: Both models failed to produce a usable audio result. Model 1 failed by repeating the prompt instructions instead of reading the target text (Instruction Following Failure). Model 2 failed by producing hallucinations and gibberish that did not match the text (Generation Quality Failure). According to the Global Considerations, if the generated audio content is extremely poor or inconsistent with the script, a score of 0 must be assigned. Since neither model successfully synthesized the correct text, they are tied at 0.
